from source import filter_expensive_items

def test_filter_expensive_items_with_string_price():
    """
    The buggy code crashes with TypeError when comparing a string price to an integer threshold.
    The fixed code should treat the string as numeric without crashing.
    """
    items = [
        {'name': 'A', 'price': 100},
        {'name': 'B', 'price': '200'}  # string that should be > 150
    ]
    threshold = 150

    # This call will raise TypeError on the buggy version.
    result = filter_expensive_items(items, threshold)

    expected = [{'name': 'B', 'price': '200'}]
    assert result == expected, f"Expected {expected}, got {result}"
